user_query:
You are a senior Diggspace workspace consultant. You have been given a company profile document and must design and build a complete digital workspace hub for this company — with no additional input from the user until you present your plan.

COMPANY PROFILE:
---
================================================================================
  COMPANY PROFILE — APPLE INC.
  Source: Public information compiled for workspace onboarding purposes
================================================================================

COMPANY OVERVIEW
----------------
Legal name:       Apple Inc.
Founded:          April 1, 1976
Headquarters:     One Apple Park Way, Cupertino, California 95014, USA
CEO:              Tim Cook (since 2011)
Employees:        ~160,000 worldwide (full-time)
Revenue (FY2024): $391 billion USD
Fiscal year end:  Last Saturday of September

Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets,
wearables, and accessories, and sells a variety of related services. It is one of
the world's most valuable companies by market capitalisation and is widely regarded
as one of the most influential technology companies in history.

BRAND IDENTITY
--------------
Apple's brand is defined by simplicity, premium quality, and human-centred design.
The visual language is clean, minimal, and typographically precise.

Primary brand colors:
  - Space Black:  #1D1D1F  (primary — used on dark backgrounds, headers)
  - Pure White:   #FFFFFF  (contrast for primary)
  - Apple Blue:   #0071E3  (interactive elements, CTAs, highlights)
  - Light Gray:   #F5F5F7  (page backgrounds, secondary surfaces)
  - Signal Red:   #FF3B30  (alerts, warnings, product accents)
  - Signal Red contrast: #FFFFFF

Typography: San Francisco (proprietary), Helvetica Neue as fallback.
Design philosophy: "Design is not just what it looks like. Design is how it works." — Steve Jobs

Website: https://www.apple.com
Careers: https://www.apple.com/careers
Newsroom: https://www.apple.com/newsroom
Privacy: https://www.apple.com/privacy
Investor Relations: https://investor.apple.com
Accessibility: https://www.apple.com/accessibility
Developer: https://developer.apple.com
Support: https://support.apple.com

Social media:
  Twitter/X:  https://twitter.com/Apple
  YouTube:    https://youtube.com/Apple
  LinkedIn:   https://linkedin.com/company/apple

BUSINESS DIVISIONS & PRODUCTS
------------------------------
Apple operates through five reportable segments:

1. iPhone — The world's leading smartphone. Introduced in 2007 by Steve Jobs.
   iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15, iPhone SE are current flagship models.
   Revenue contribution: ~52% of total revenue.

2. Mac — Personal computing line including MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, Mac mini,
   iMac, Mac Studio, and Mac Pro. Runs macOS. Transitioned to Apple Silicon (M-series)
   in 2020. Revenue: ~8% of total.

3. iPad — Tablet lineup: iPad Pro, iPad Air, iPad mini, iPad. Runs iPadOS.
   Revenue: ~4% of total.

4. Wearables, Home & Accessories — Apple Watch, AirPods, AirPods Pro, AirPods Max,
   HomePod, Apple TV, MagSafe accessories, Beats products.
   Revenue: ~10% of total.

5. Services — The fastest growing segment. Includes:
   - App Store (global marketplace, 36 million developers)
   - Apple Music (100 million songs)
   - Apple TV+ (original content streaming)
   - Apple Arcade (game subscription)
   - iCloud+ (storage and privacy features)
   - Apple Pay & Apple Card
   - AppleCare+ (extended warranty and support)
   Revenue: ~26% of total, ~$96 billion annually.

OPERATIONS & RETAIL
--------------------
Apple operates 516 retail stores in 25 countries. Apple Store employees are called
Specialists, Geniuses (at the Genius Bar for technical support), and Creative Pros.
The retail experience is a core part of Apple's brand strategy.

Manufacturing is primarily handled by contract manufacturers in China (Foxconn,
Pegatron), with increasing production in India and Vietnam.

Supply chain is managed from Cupertino with deep supplier relationships across 43
countries. Apple publishes an annual Supplier Responsibility Report.

PEOPLE & CULTURE
----------------
Apple employs ~160,000 full-time employees globally. The culture is characterised by:
- Secrecy and confidentiality (employees often don't know what other teams are building)
- Deep ownership mentality — small teams with high accountability
- "The best or nothing" standards
- Diverse and inclusive hiring practices (Apple releases an annual Diversity Report)
- Benefits: comprehensive health coverage, RSU equity grants, employee product discounts,
  education reimbursement, free onsite health clinics, parental leave (up to 16 weeks)

Employee resource groups: Black@Apple, Pride@Apple, Women@Apple, Veterans@Apple, Accessibility@Apple

2024 Glassdoor rating: 4.2/5
LinkedIn Top Companies: Consistently ranked in top 10

HR contacts: hr@apple.com
Careers portal: https://www.apple.com/careers/us/

INNOVATION & R&D
-----------------
Apple spends approximately $29 billion annually on R&D (FY2024).
Key ongoing R&D areas:
- Apple Silicon (M-series, A-series chips) — custom ARM-based processors
- Vision Pro / visionOS — spatial computing platform (launched January 2024)
- Health & fitness sensing (Apple Watch, research apps)
- Machine learning and on-device AI (Apple Intelligence, announced 2024)
- Autonomous systems (Project Titan — vehicle technology, status unclear)
- Privacy-preserving technologies (differential privacy, on-device processing)

Apple holds approximately 75,000 patents worldwide.

CORPORATE RESPONSIBILITY & ESG
--------------------------------
Environment:
- Carbon neutral across corporate operations since 2020
- Goal: carbon neutral across entire supply chain and product lifecycle by 2030
- 100% renewable energy for all Apple facilities
- Recycled materials in products (recycled aluminium, rare earth elements)
- Apple publishes an annual Environmental Progress Report

Privacy:
- Core brand value: "Privacy is a fundamental human right"
- App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework
- On-device processing to minimise data leaving the device
- Privacy labels in App Store

Social impact:
- $200 million Racial Equity and Justice Initiative (REJI)
- Apple Impact Accelerator for minority-owned businesses
- ConnectED: donated technology to schools

Governance:
- Board of Directors: 8 members, majority independent
- Chair: Arthur Levinson
- Annual proxy statement published at investor.apple.com
- Named to Dow Jones Sustainability Index

RECENT MAJOR ANNOUNCEMENTS (2024)
-----------------------------------
- Apple Intelligence: AI features integrated across iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS Sequoia.
  Uses on-device and Private Cloud Compute models.
- Vision Pro: Spatial computing headset launched in January 2024 at $3,499.
  visionOS 2 announced at WWDC 2024.
- iPhone 16 lineup: Announced September 2024. Camera Control button, A18 chip.
- M4 chip family: M4, M4 Pro, M4 Max announced across MacBook Pro, Mac mini, iMac.
- Apple Watch Series 10: Thinner design, larger display, faster charging.
- Siri upgrade: Deep integration with Apple Intelligence and ChatGPT.
- EU Digital Markets Act compliance: Opened App Store to alternative marketplaces in EU.
- Antitrust proceedings: DOJ antitrust lawsuit filed March 2024.

FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS (FY2024)
------------------------------
Revenue:          $391.0B  (+2% YoY)
Gross margin:     46.2%
Operating income: $123.2B
Net income:       $93.7B
Cash & securities: $153.0B
Debt:             $101.3B
EPS (diluted):    $6.11
Shares buyback:   $95B repurchased in FY2024
Dividend:         $0.25/share per quarter

Stock: AAPL — listed on NASDAQ
Market cap: ~$3.4 trillion (as of late 2024)

OFFICES & KEY LOCATIONS
------------------------
Apple Park (HQ):      Cupertino, CA — the "spaceship" campus, opened 2017
One Infinite Loop:    Cupertino, CA — original HQ, now secondary
Austin, TX:           Major campus, 5,000+ employees, Mac Pro manufactured here
Seattle, WA:          AI and machine learning hub
New York, NY:         Multiple offices + flagship 5th Avenue store
Boston, MA:           Research lab
London, UK:           Battersea Power Station (6,000 employees, opened 2022)
Munich, Germany:      European chip and wireless engineering hub
Shenzhen, China:      Supply chain management hub
Tokyo, Japan:         Regional HQ for Asia Pacific

LEGAL & COMPLIANCE
------------------
Apple faces ongoing regulatory scrutiny globally:
- EU Digital Markets Act — gatekeeper designation, App Store rules
- DOJ antitrust (US) — smartphone monopoly allegations (filed March 2024)
- Competition investigations in UK, Germany, South Korea, Japan, India
- Data privacy regulators: GDPR compliance in Europe, CCPA in California

Apple's legal team is one of the largest in the technology industry.
Chief Legal Officer: Katherine Adams

================================================================================
  END OF COMPANY PROFILE
================================================================================
---

PHASE 0 — ANALYSE & DESIGN (no tools yet)
Read the profile carefully. Then produce a structured workspace design proposal:

1. SCOPE CONFIG
   - Proposed scope name (professional, matches the brand)
   - Welcome message (1-2 sentences, brand voice, specific to this company)
   - Primary color + contrast, secondary color, warn color + contrast
     (derive from brand identity section; use exact hex values if given,
      otherwise reason from the brand description)
   - Number of highlights, carousel on/off, style
   - Footer columns (3 columns, derive from website links in the profile)
   - Social links (derive from social media section)

2. CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE
   Design 5-8 channels that reflect how this company actually operates.
   Do NOT use generic names like 'News' or 'HR'. Use names that fit this
   company's language, culture, and structure. For each channel:
   - Name (specific to the company)
   - Purpose (one sentence)
   - Type: public / private / corporate
   - Color (hex, derived from brand palette or business division color)
   - Sticky: yes/no (only the 1-2 most critical channels)
   - Description (2-3 sentences of real HTML content)
   - Tabs: Articles / Pages / both

3. WELCOME ARTICLE
   Propose a title, the channel it goes in, and 3-4 paragraph HTML body
   that sounds like it was written by this company's internal comms team.

Present this full design proposal clearly. Then ask:
'Does this architecture look right, or would you like to adjust anything
before I start building?'

STOP — wait for approval before calling any tool.

PHASE 1 — BUILD SCOPE (after design is approved)
a. Call check_connection.
b. Call create_scope with ALL fields from the approved design — nothing null:
   name, welcome_message, channel_creation_by_admins_only=true,
   primary_color, primary_color_contrast, secondary_color,
   warn_color, warn_color_contrast,
   number_of_highlights, highlights_carousel, highlights_style,
   footer_block_links (all columns), navigation_links (social links).
c. Report: scope name, id, route. STOP — wait for confirmation.

PHASE 2 — BUILD CHANNELS (after Phase 1 confirmed)
Create every channel from the approved design, in priority order.
For each create_channel call use the full description HTML, correct type,
color, sticky, tabs, and default_role where appropriate.
No channel should have a null or empty description.
After all channels are created, present a summary table:
  Channel | id | type | sticky
STOP — wait for confirmation before Phase 3.

PHASE 3 — WELCOME ARTICLE (after Phase 2 confirmed)
Publish the welcome article from the approved design using create_article:
  status=Published, is_sticky=true, is_main_highlight=true, notify=true.
The body must be polished HTML that sounds like internal comms at this company.

PHASE 4 — FINAL HANDOVER
Deliver a clean summary:
  Company: [name]
  Scope: [name] — id: [id] — route: [route]
  Channels created: [count] — [list with ids]
  Welcome article: [title] — id: [id]
  Brand colors applied: primary [hex] / secondary [hex] / warn [hex]

Then ask: 'Would you like to refine anything — rename a channel, adjust colors,
or add additional channels for specific teams or regions?'

RULES THROUGHOUT
- Derive everything from the profile. Do not invent information not present in it.
- Never use placeholder text, null descriptions, or generic channel names.
- If the profile mentions specific teams, divisions, or geographies, reflect them.
- Confirm before each phase. If a phase fails, stop and report what succeeded.
- The final workspace should feel like it was built specifically for this company,
  not like a template with the company name swapped in.
final_answer_preview:
PHASE 0 — ANALYSE & DESIGN

Here is a complete, brand-aligned plan for Apple Inc.’s Diggspace workspace hub. I’ve derived every element directly from the company profile you provided and shaped it to feel like Apple’s distinct design-led, confidential culture.

1) SCOPE CONFIG

- Proposed scope name
  - Apple Corporate Hub

- Welcome message (1–2 sentences, brand voice)
  - Welcome to the Apple Co
